meta.title: Agent Control Panel
meta.subtitle: Portable operator view for a Claude Code stack.
meta.operatorNote: Replace this file with your real stack metadata and operating rules.

status.label: Browser lane
status.note: Use the browser-native lane while the page stays inside a stable DOM surface.

status.label: Desktop lane
status.note: Use the desktop lane for native UI, browser chrome, file pickers, and visible-truth verification.

status.label: Dynamic lane
status.note: Use the dynamic lane only when normal desktop automation is too static.

status.label: Snapshot model
status.note: This panel stays portable because the UI is generic and adapters supply the data.

lanes.name: Browser-native lane
lanes.summary: Primary route for healthy webpage DOM work.
useWhen: Stable DOM tasks such as navigation, reading, forms, and normal clicks.
Use the fastest browser-native tooling your client actually has.
lanes.escalateWhen: Escalate when native browser UI, file pickers, no-op actions, or visible-state mismatches appear.

lanes.name: Desktop-control lane
lanes.summary: Truth layer for native dialogs, browser chrome, weak UIA, and final visual verification.
useWhen: Native browser UI, file pickers, Windows dialogs, and weak-UIA apps.
When the screen must arbitrate whether the task really finished.
lanes.escalateWhen: Escalate again only when the target is moving, animated, drag-validated, or timing-sensitive.

lanes.name: Dynamic-control lane
lanes.summary: High-frequency watch-think-act path for motion-heavy or timing-sensitive surfaces.
useWhen: Canvas surfaces, drag validation, game-like UI, or short hit windows.
Use only when the browser or desktop lane is too static.
lanes.escalateWhen: Drop back to the browser or desktop lane as soon as a cheaper route is healthy again.

principles.title: Visible truth wins
principles.body: If the tool says success but the screen does not, trust the visible state.

principles.title: Escalate deliberately
principles.body: Use the lightest route that can still complete the task, then escalate only when reality demands it.

principles.title: Recovery is part of the system
principles.body: Browser stalls, native blockers, focus drift, and timing misses are normal; the stack should recover instead of guessing.

principles.title: Do not trap knowledge in chat
principles.body: Stable workflows, limits, and helper behavior should be written back into docs, scripts, and project-local notes.

prompts.title: Local universal startup prompt
prompts.summary: Use when a new local session must synchronize against the current stack before doing real work.
prompts.body: Read the local foundation docs, verify the machine state, map the current lanes, and only then start the task.

prompts.title: Agent-specific startup prompt
prompts.summary: Use when a session is clearly tied to one client family and should stay focused on that stack.
prompts.body: Keep the default browser route pure, keep desktop rescue ready, and avoid dragging unrelated sidecars into generic work.

prompts.title: Project-fork startup prompt
prompts.summary: Use only after sync is complete and the session should evolve independently.
prompts.body: Fork the main foundation into a project-local stack, write new learnings there first, and only promote back on purpose.

helpers.name: browser preflight
helpers.summary: Checks for obvious native blockers before the first serious browser action.

helpers.name: browser stall rescue
helpers.summary: First recovery step after a no-op, timeout, or localized native prompt.

helpers.name: visual audit
helpers.summary: Compares tool output with the visible screen when they disagree.

helpers.name: stable input
helpers.summary: Clipboard-first verified input for IME-heavy or fragile text fields.

helpers.name: dynamic surface pipeline
helpers.summary: Escalation path for moving or timing-sensitive surfaces.

skills.name: browser-failsafe-rescue
skills.summary: Recover from browser stalls and native blockers.

skills.name: dynamic-control-escalation
skills.summary: Decide when to escalate into the high-frequency lane.

skills.name: windows-desktop-precision
skills.summary: Handle weak-UIA surfaces and small targets more precisely.

docs.title: Foundation entry
docs.summary: The top-level operator and routing docs for your stack.

docs.title: Failsafe guide
docs.summary: Rules for browser stalls, native blockers, and screen-first verification.

docs.title: Dynamic lane guide
docs.summary: Escalation rules for motion, timing, and drag-sensitive work.

adapters.name: Codex adapter
adapters.summary: Builds a snapshot from a local Codex stack by reading docs, config, and installed skills.

adapters.name: Claude Code adapter
adapters.summary: Builds the same snapshot shape from a JSON config for Claude Code or adjacent local-agent stacks.
